title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Momentum
thesis: Rapid growth in public EV charging infrastructure signals increased capex and demand for electric vehicle adoption, indicating macro tailwinds for industrials and materials. EV charger buildout requires metals, electrical infrastructure, and industrial construction—direct XLI exposure.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region
signal_logic: If weekly net new chargers added exceeds 1500 units (7-day rolling average) above the prior month's median
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly net new chargers added exceeds 1500 units (7-day rolling average) above the prior month's median Exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly charger additions drop below 1200 units
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional charger buildout fluctuates seasonally and by policy; multiple regions typically add >1500 chargers per week during growth cycles.

data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region

schema.behavior: Rapid growth in public EV charging infrastructure signals increased capex and demand for electric vehicle adoption, indicating macro tailwinds for industrials and materials.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger buildout requires metals, electrical infrastructure, and industrial construction—direct XLI exposure.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net new chargers added exceeds 1500 units (7-day rolling average) above the prior month's median
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly charger additions drop below 1200 units

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional charger buildout fluctuates seasonally and by policy; multiple regions typically add >1500 chargers per week during growth cycles.

title: Jobless Claims Shock Reversal Trade
thesis: When initial jobless claims spike above 2-standard-deviations of the 8-week rolling mean, a mean-reversion bounce typically follows within 2-3 weeks as temporary layoffs resolve. Claims shock reversals signal labor market stabilization, boosting consumer discretionary spending confidence.
data_sources: FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly)
signal_logic: When weekly initial claims exceed (8-week rolling mean + 2*stdev) AND prior week was not already elevated
entry_exit: Entry: When weekly initial claims exceed (8-week rolling mean + 2*stdev) AND prior week was not already elevated Exit: After claims return to rolling mean or 12 trading days elapse
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly claims data is volatile; seasonal layoffs and temporary shocks occur 3-5 times per year, triggering mean-reversion signals.

data.source: FRED series ICSA (initial jobless claims weekly)

schema.behavior: When initial jobless claims spike above 2-standard-deviations of the 8-week rolling mean, a mean-reversion bounce typically follows within 2-3 weeks as temporary layoffs resolve.

market_impact.rationale: Claims shock reversals signal labor market stabilization, boosting consumer discretionary spending confidence.

trade_logic.entry: When weekly initial claims exceed (8-week rolling mean + 2*stdev) AND prior week was not already elevated
trade_logic.exit: After claims return to rolling mean or 12 trading days elapse

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly claims data is volatile; seasonal layoffs and temporary shocks occur 3-5 times per year, triggering mean-reversion signals.

title: Weather Shock Agricultural Input Demand Spike
thesis: Severe drought (>2 weeks below -1.5σ precipitation) or unexpected freeze events trigger emergency demand for crop inputs, fertilizers, and irrigation—driving materials and industrial stocks. Ag input shocks drive demand for fertilizers, chemicals, and equipment—core XLB constituents.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily precipitation and temperature anomalies for major US agricultural regions
signal_logic: If 5-day rolling precipitation anomaly for Corn Belt drops below -1.5σ OR temperature swings >8°C below seasonal norm for 3+ consecutive days
entry_exit: Entry: If 5-day rolling precipitation anomaly for Corn Belt drops below -1.5σ OR temperature swings >8°C below seasonal norm for 3+ consecutive days Exit: After 8 trading days or when weather normalizes toward seasonal mean
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily precipitation and temperature anomalies for major US agricultural regions via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility is frequent; drought/freeze shocks occur 4-6 times per growing season across US regions.

schema.idea: Weather Shock Agricultural Input Demand Spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily precipitation and temperature anomalies for major US agricultural regions

schema.behavior: Severe drought (>2 weeks below -1.5σ precipitation) or unexpected freeze events trigger emergency demand for crop inputs, fertilizers, and irrigation—driving materials and industrial stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Ag input shocks drive demand for fertilizers, chemicals, and equipment—core XLB constituents.

trade_logic.entry: If 5-day rolling precipitation anomaly for Corn Belt drops below -1.5σ OR temperature swings >8°C below seasonal norm for 3+ consecutive days
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when weather normalizes toward seasonal mean

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility is frequent; drought/freeze shocks occur 4-6 times per growing season across US regions.

title: Earthquake Damage Insurance Claim Anticipation
thesis: Significant earthquake clusters (>3 events magnitude 4.0+ within 7 days in same region) trigger insurance loss recognition and reconstruction demand—benefiting financials and industrials. Immediate insurance claim spikes hurt financial sector valuations; property & casualty insurers face margin pressure.
data_sources: USGS Earthquake Activity API (daily magnitude >4.0 events in insurable regions)
signal_logic: If 7-day rolling count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes in CA/OR/WA exceeds 3 events
entry_exit: Entry: If 7-day rolling count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes in CA/OR/WA exceeds 3 events Exit: After 15 trading days or when seismic activity normalizes to <1 event per week
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS Earthquake Activity API (daily magnitude >4.0 events in insurable regions) via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake clusters occur regularly along US fault lines; 2-3 trading opportunities per quarter in seismically active states.

data.source: USGS Earthquake Activity API (daily magnitude >4.0 events in insurable regions)

schema.behavior: Significant earthquake clusters (>3 events magnitude 4.0+ within 7 days in same region) trigger insurance loss recognition and reconstruction demand—benefiting financials and industrials.

market_impact.rationale: Immediate insurance claim spikes hurt financial sector valuations; property & casualty insurers face margin pressure.

trade_logic.entry: If 7-day rolling count of magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes in CA/OR/WA exceeds 3 events
trade_logic.exit: After 15 trading days or when seismic activity normalizes to <1 event per week

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake clusters occur regularly along US fault lines; 2-3 trading opportunities per quarter in seismically active states.

title: Google Trends Surge In Unemployment Benefits Search Volume
thesis: Sharp week-over-week spikes in unemployment benefits search volume (>40% increase) precede negative consumer sentiment and spending cuts, signaling economic stress. Consumer discretionary spending declines as households anticipate or experience job loss.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'how to apply for unemployment'
signal_logic: If weekly search volume index exceeds prior 4-week average by >40% AND prior week was not already elevated
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search volume index exceeds prior 4-week average by >40% AND prior week was not already elevated Exit: After 10 trading days or when search volume returns to 4-week baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'how to apply for unemployment' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Google Trends search spikes correlate with labor market weakness; seasonal layoffs and recession fears trigger 3-4 signals per year.

schema.idea: Google Trends Surge in 'Unemployment Benefits' Search Volume

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'how to apply for unemployment'

schema.behavior: Sharp week-over-week spikes in unemployment benefits search volume (>40% increase) precede negative consumer sentiment and spending cuts, signaling economic stress.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary spending declines as households anticipate or experience job loss.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search volume index exceeds prior 4-week average by >40% AND prior week was not already elevated
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when search volume returns to 4-week baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Google Trends search spikes correlate with labor market weakness; seasonal layoffs and recession fears trigger 3-4 signals per year.

title: Airline Load Factor Compression Signal
thesis: When monthly load factor drops >2% from prior month, airlines cut capacity, reducing fuel demand and signaling travel demand weakness—bearish for airlines and energy. Lower airline load factors reduce jet fuel consumption and signal reduced consumer travel spending.
data_sources: BTS Airline Load Factor monthly average (via html_table scrape)
signal_logic: If latest month's load factor is >2 percentage points below prior month AND below 12-month rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: If latest month's load factor is >2 percentage points below prior month AND below 12-month rolling average Exit: After 15 trading days or when load factor recovers toward 12-month mean
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS Airline Load Factor monthly average (via html_table scrape) via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal capacity adjustments occur quarterly; holiday travel shifts and recession fears trigger load factor compressions 3-4 times per year.

data.source: BTS Airline Load Factor monthly average (via html_table scrape)

schema.behavior: When monthly load factor drops >2% from prior month, airlines cut capacity, reducing fuel demand and signaling travel demand weakness—bearish for airlines and energy.

market_impact.rationale: Lower airline load factors reduce jet fuel consumption and signal reduced consumer travel spending.

trade_logic.entry: If latest month's load factor is >2 percentage points below prior month AND below 12-month rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 15 trading days or when load factor recovers toward 12-month mean

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal capacity adjustments occur quarterly; holiday travel shifts and recession fears trigger load factor compressions 3-4 times per year.

title: Corporate Debt Maturity Wall Rss Count Surge
thesis: When daily RSS mentions of corporate debt maturity risks spike >3x the rolling 30-day mean, media focus on refinancing pressure builds, signaling near-term credit stress and equity weakness. Debt refinancing anxiety pressures financial sector valuations and bank credit portfolios.
data_sources: RSS feeds from financial news (Bloomberg, Reuters, WSJ) filtered for 'debt maturity' and 'refinance risk' mentions
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for 'debt maturity' OR 'refinance risk' exceeds 3x the 30-day rolling median
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for 'debt maturity' OR 'refinance risk' exceeds 3x the 30-day rolling median Exit: After 7 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feeds from financial news (Bloomberg, Reuters, WSJ) filtered for 'debt maturity' and 'refinance risk' mentions via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Debt walls and refinancing cycles trigger news surges 2-3 times per quarter; Q1 and Q4 earnings-driven volatility ensures frequent signals.

schema.idea: Corporate Debt Maturity Wall RSS Count Surge

data.source: RSS feeds from financial news (Bloomberg, Reuters, WSJ) filtered for 'debt maturity' and 'refinance risk' mentions

schema.behavior: When daily RSS mentions of corporate debt maturity risks spike >3x the rolling 30-day mean, media focus on refinancing pressure builds, signaling near-term credit stress and equity weakness.

market_impact.rationale: Debt refinancing anxiety pressures financial sector valuations and bank credit portfolios.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS count for 'debt maturity' OR 'refinance risk' exceeds 3x the 30-day rolling median
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Debt walls and refinancing cycles trigger news surges 2-3 times per quarter; Q1 and Q4 earnings-driven volatility ensures frequent signals.

title: Eia Natural Gas Storage Build Momentum Divergence
thesis: When weekly gas storage builds accelerate >20% faster than the prior 4-week rolling average (suggesting oversupply), natural gas prices compress, hurting energy producer margins—bearish energy. Rapid storage accumulation signals weak demand or oversupply, driving down nat gas prices and producer revenue.
data_sources: EIA Weekly Natural Gas Inventory Data (via FRED or EIA API)
signal_logic: If weekly storage build exceeds 4-week rolling average by >20% AND prior 2 weeks showed accelerating builds
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly storage build exceeds 4-week rolling average by >20% AND prior 2 weeks showed accelerating builds Exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly build rate normalizes below 4-week mean
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use EIA Weekly Natural Gas Inventory Data (via FRED or EIA API) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal gas storage cycles and weather-driven demand shifts trigger build acceleration signals 8-12 times per year.

schema.idea: EIA Natural Gas Storage Build Momentum Divergence

data.source: EIA Weekly Natural Gas Inventory Data (via FRED or EIA API)

schema.behavior: When weekly gas storage builds accelerate >20% faster than the prior 4-week rolling average (suggesting oversupply), natural gas prices compress, hurting energy producer margins—bearish energy.

market_impact.rationale: Rapid storage accumulation signals weak demand or oversupply, driving down nat gas prices and producer revenue.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly storage build exceeds 4-week rolling average by >20% AND prior 2 weeks showed accelerating builds
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly build rate normalizes below 4-week mean

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal gas storage cycles and weather-driven demand shifts trigger build acceleration signals 8-12 times per year.

title: Hospital Admission Rate Rss Spike Non-covid
thesis: Sudden spikes in RSS mentions of hospital admission surges (>2x rolling average) signal seasonal flu, RSV, or accident clusters, driving healthcare utilization and equipment demand. Hospital admission surges boost pharmaceutical demand, diagnostic testing, and medical device utilization—benefiting healthcare sector.
data_sources: RSS feeds from health department and hospital network news mentioning 'admission surge' or 'bed occupancy'
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for hospital admission-related terms exceeds 2x the 20-day rolling median
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for hospital admission-related terms exceeds 2x the 20-day rolling median Exit: After 8 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feeds from health department and hospital network news mentioning 'admission surge' or 'bed occupancy' via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal flu/RSV surges occur Oct-Mar annually; unexpected accident clusters and disease spikes trigger signals 4-6 times per year.

data.source: RSS feeds from health department and hospital network news mentioning 'admission surge' or 'bed occupancy'

market_impact.rationale: Hospital admission surges boost pharmaceutical demand, diagnostic testing, and medical device utilization—benefiting healthcare sector.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS count for hospital admission-related terms exceeds 2x the 20-day rolling median
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when RSS mention count returns to rolling baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal flu/RSV surges occur Oct-Mar annually; unexpected accident clusters and disease spikes trigger signals 4-6 times per year.

title: Fred Core Cpi Expectation Reversal Divergence
thesis: When 5-year inflation expectations drop >15 basis points in a single week after 4+ weeks of stable/rising expectations, it signals a demand destruction shock or disinflation surprise—bullish for long-duration assets. Collapsing inflation expectations lower discount rates and boost tech/growth valuations.
data_sources: FRED series MMNRNJ (5-year inflation expectation) weekly aggregation
signal_logic: If weekly change in 5-year inflation expectations is <-15bps AND prior 4 weeks showed stable/positive change
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly change in 5-year inflation expectations is <-15bps AND prior 4 weeks showed stable/positive change Exit: After 12 trading days or when expectations stabilize/rebound >10bps
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series MMNRNJ (5-year inflation expectation) weekly aggregation via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Inflation expectations shift with Fed policy and CPI surprises; directional reversals occur 3-4 times per quarter during volatile periods.

schema.idea: FRED Core CPI Expectation Reversal Divergence

data.source: FRED series MMNRNJ (5-year inflation expectation) weekly aggregation

schema.behavior: When 5-year inflation expectations drop >15 basis points in a single week after 4+ weeks of stable/rising expectations, it signals a demand destruction shock or disinflation surprise—bullish for long-duration assets.

market_impact.rationale: Collapsing inflation expectations lower discount rates and boost tech/growth valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly change in 5-year inflation expectations is <-15bps AND prior 4 weeks showed stable/positive change
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or when expectations stabilize/rebound >10bps

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Inflation expectations shift with Fed policy and CPI surprises; directional reversals occur 3-4 times per quarter during volatile periods.
